purpose: Bridge between IDE workspaces (folders opened in the Code Explorer) and dedicated dashboards. Stores the workspaceId→dashboardId mapping in localStorage so IDE chat context survives restarts. Provides reverse lookup (which workspace owns a dashboard?), a 'is dashboard linked to IDE?' check, label decoration for sidebar listings, and a deduped-async createWorkspaceDashboard() that uses an in-flight promise map to prevent multiple concurrent useEffect hooks from spawning duplicate dashboards.
gotchas: createWorkspaceDashboard's _inFlight Map is a module-scoped guard — concurrent calls for the same workspaceId return the same promise. Without this, two simultaneous useEffect mounts would each create a separate dashboard and orphan one.

Re-check happens both before AND after awaiting the in-flight slot (existing → return, then promise → await getWorkspaceDashboard(workspaceId) again) — prevents race when multiple callers hit createWorkspaceDashboard at once.
isIdeDashboard does an O(N) linear scan over all workspace mappings — fine at typical N (<10) but quadratic if called inside a render loop over dashboards.
removeWorkspaceDashboard is for explicit unlinking — closing a workspace tab in IDEView does NOT call this; the mapping persists across sessions intentionally.
Storage key 'synapse-ide-workspace-dashboards' is distinct from AppContext's IDE_WORKSPACES_KEY ('synapse-ide-workspaces') — workspaces and their dashboard links are persisted separately.

Disk-sync is NOT done here — workspace-dashboard mappings are localStorage-only. Wiping localStorage orphans workspaces from their dashboards (the dashboards still exist on disk, just lose their IDE context binding).
patterns: In-flight promise map pattern — Map<key, Promise> deduplicates concurrent async creators. Reusable for any async-create-once workflow.
Pure-localStorage helpers + async create-with-IPC pattern — same shape as dashboardProjects.js.
